name: Raised Sausage Meat and Egg Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease an oblong loaf or cake tin.
2. Roll out thawed pastry into a large oblong about the thickness of a penny. Cut off a piece of pastry to fit the top of the loaf and reserve it. Use the remainder to line the base and sides of the tin.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine sausage meat, onion, sage, and breadcrumbs. Bind with beaten egg, reserving a little for glazing the pastry. Turn the mixture into the pastry-lined tin and press hard-boiled eggs down in the center, covering them with sausage meat.
4. Place the pastry lid on top. Trim the corners and seal the edges. Roll out the trimmings and cut decorative "leaves."
5. Make a small hole in the center of the pie to allow steam to escape. Flute the edges of the pastry. Brush with the reserved beaten egg, then place the pastry leaves in position and brush these as well.
6. Bake in a pre-heated, hot oven (425°F, gas mark 6) for 10 to 15 minutes until the pastry has set. Then reduce the heat to moderate (375°F, gas mark 4) and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes.
7. Serve hot with cooked vegetables or serve cold with salad or pickles.
